page: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual server featuring an AMD EPYC processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs, which map to 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes a 16 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a 180 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates a distinct performance split. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% of tested servers, while multi-core CPU performance is average. However, the instance exhibits significant bottlenecks in memory-intensive operations. Both read and write large-block uncached memory bandwidths are poor, falling into the bottom 10%. This limitation is reflected in poor PassMark memory and database operations scores. Cryptographic performance is average, but static web serving throughput and LLM inference speeds for small models remain weak.

Given its shared CPU allocation and resource density, this server represents a budget-friendly option for compute-focused tasks that do not rely on high memory throughput. It is well-suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and general-purpose development environments. It is not recommended for high-throughput database hosting or memory-heavy workloads due to the documented memory bandwidth constraints.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server configured with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, the processor supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions. Benchmarks reveal a split performance profile: single-core CPU execution is strong, ranking in the top 25%, while multi-core performance remains average. However, the instance exhibits poor large-block uncached memory bandwidth and database operations, both landing in the bottom 10%. With a shared CPU allocation, this server provides a cost-efficient option for compute tasks with low memory-throughput requirements. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded applications, and general-purpose development workloads rather than intensive database or memory-heavy operations.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd is a High Performance server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 180 GB NVMe SSD. It offers strong single-core CPU performance but faces memory bandwidth limitations.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 180 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A shared AMD EPYC virtual server delivering strong single-core compute performance paired with local NVMe storage for budget-conscious workloads.

Bundled 180 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Tradeoffs include poor large-block uncached memory bandwidth
